title: American Creamy Coleslaw

subs.name: half green cabbage and half red (purple) cabbage for a more colourful version
lemon juice (nimbu) in equal quantity — gives a fresher, more citrus-forward dressing
honey or jaggery (gur) dissolved in the vinegar for a slightly earthier sweetness

steps.text: Shred the cabbage as finely as possible using a sharp knife or a mandoline. Grate the carrot on the large holes of a box grater. If using onion, slice paper-thin.
Toss the shredded vegetables with half a teaspoon of salt in a large bowl. Leave for 10 minutes — the salt draws out excess moisture so the dressing clings rather than pools at the bottom.
Squeeze the vegetables firmly with your hands to press out as much water as possible, then drain. This step is what keeps the coleslaw from turning watery.
Whisk together the vinegar, sugar, oil and black pepper in a small bowl until the sugar dissolves. Taste and adjust — it should be sharp, lightly sweet and well-seasoned.

Pour the dressing over the squeezed vegetables and toss thoroughly to coat. Taste and correct for salt and vinegar. Chill for at least 30 minutes before serving — the texture improves significantly as it sits.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: vinegar → lemon juice (nimbu) in equal quantity — gives a fresher, more citrus-forward dressing; cabbage → half green cabbage and half red (purple) cabbage for a more colourful version; sugar → honey or jaggery (gur) dissolved in the vinegar for a slightly earthier sweetness
indian_kitchen_adaptation: No special equipment needed — a sharp knife or grater and a bowl are all you need. White vinegar (sirka) or apple cider vinegar is available at any supermarket. For a desi twist, add a pinch of chaat masala and a squeeze of nimbu. This works beautifully alongside any fried snack, not just American dishes.
